description:
You are an automation-workflow specialist who has been asked to create a daily summary from yesterday’s service log.

  Existing data:
    • A text file already exists at /home/user/logs/automation_events.log.  
      All entries are from a single day (2024-04-12) and follow this exact pattern  
      <YYYY-MM-DD> <hh:mm:ss> <LEVEL> <Component>: <EVENT_TYPE> id=<ID> [extra-fields]  

      Examples
        2024-04-12 10:15:23 INFO  TaskScheduler: TASK_START   id=123 type=backup
        2024-04-12 10:15:25 ERROR TaskProcessor: TASK_FAIL    id=123 reason=NO_SPACE
        2024-04-12 10:20:30 INFO  TaskProcessor: TASK_SUCCESS id=124 duration=29s  

      Each line is whitespace-separated exactly as shown (single spaces, no tabs).

  Goal:
    1. Analyse /home/user/logs/automation_events.log.
    2. Create the directory /home/user/analysis (if it does not already exist).
    3. Inside that directory write the file   /home/user/analysis/daily_summary_2024-04-12.txt
       in the format described below (all lines must end with a UNIX newline, “\n”).

       REQUIRED FILE FORMAT (checked by automated tests – every space and colon matters):
         Line 1 :  DATE: 2024-04-12
         Line 2 :  (blank line)
         Line 3 :  TASK_START: <count_of_TASK_START_lines>
         Line 4 :  TASK_SUCCESS: <count_of_TASK_SUCCESS_lines>
         Line 5 :  TASK_FAIL: <count_of_TASK_FAIL_lines>
         Line 6 :  (blank line)
         Line 7 :  FAILURE_REASONS:
         Line 8+: one line per distinct failure reason, listed in
                 alphabetical order of the reason string, each line
                 formatted exactly as
                   <REASON>=<count>
                 Do NOT add spaces around the “=”.
                 When there are several reasons you must output one per line.
         After the last reason line: (blank line)
         Final line :  MOST_RECENT_FAILURE: <id> <timestamp> <reason>
                       where
                         <id>        is the numeric id of the latest TASK_FAIL
                                     event in chronological order
                         <timestamp> is the timestamp from that log line
                                     in the exact form YYYY-MM-DD hh:mm:ss
                         <reason>    is the reason field of that failure

         The file must finish with a trailing newline.

    4. Display the whole contents of the summary file to standard output
       so that a human reviewer can see it immediately.
       Simply “cat”-ing the file after you create it is sufficient.

  Hints & constraints:
    • Use only standard GNU/Linux user-level tools (grep, awk, sed, sort, cut, etc.).
    • The entire workflow can be completed in roughly 5-10 shell commands.
    • You do NOT need root or sudo privileges.
    • Be sure to preserve exact spacing/capitalisation shown above.
    • The automated grader will fail if even one line or one space is incorrect,
      so it is safest to build the file with echo/printf or
      redirect the output of text-processing commands directly.

  No other files should be modified or created.
  Only /home/user/analysis/daily_summary_2024-04-12.txt will be graded.
truth:
/home/user/logs/automation_events.log contents (16 lines):

    2024-04-12 08:00:01 INFO TaskScheduler: TASK_START id=120 type=cleanup
    2024-04-12 08:00:03 INFO TaskProcessor: TASK_SUCCESS id=120 duration=2s
    2024-04-12 09:15:00 INFO TaskScheduler: TASK_START id=121 type=backup
    2024-04-12 09:15:20 ERROR TaskProcessor: TASK_FAIL id=121 reason=NO_SPACE
    2024-04-12 09:45:10 INFO TaskScheduler: TASK_START id=122 type=deploy
    2024-04-12 09:45:45 INFO TaskProcessor: TASK_SUCCESS id=122 duration=35s
    2024-04-12 10:05:11 INFO TaskScheduler: TASK_START id=123 type=backup
    2024-04-12 10:05:40 ERROR TaskProcessor: TASK_FAIL id=123 reason=TIMEOUT
    2024-04-12 11:30:04 INFO TaskScheduler: TASK_START id=124 type=cleanup
    2024-04-12 11:30:05 ERROR TaskProcessor: TASK_FAIL id=124 reason=PERMISSION_DENIED
    2024-04-12 12:00:22 INFO TaskScheduler: TASK_START id=125 type=backup
    2024-04-12 12:00:52 INFO TaskProcessor: TASK_SUCCESS id=125 duration=30s
    2024-04-12 13:20:30 INFO TaskScheduler: TASK_START id=126 type=deploy
    2024-04-12 13:21:10 INFO TaskProcessor: TASK_SUCCESS id=126 duration=40s
    2024-04-12 14:44:55 INFO TaskScheduler: TASK_START id=127 type=cleanup
    2024-04-12 14:45:01 ERROR TaskProcessor: TASK_FAIL id=127 reason=NO_SPACE

  Expected generated file /home/user/analysis/daily_summary_2024-04-12.txt:

    DATE: 2024-04-12

    TASK_START: 8
    TASK_SUCCESS: 4
    TASK_FAIL: 4

    FAILURE_REASONS:
    NO_SPACE=2
    PERMISSION_DENIED=1
    TIMEOUT=1

    MOST_RECENT_FAILURE: 127 2024-04-12 14:45:01 NO_SPACE

    (note: the file ends with a newline)

  The grading script will:
    • Check that the summary file exists.
    • Verify exact byte-for-byte match with the block above, including final newline.
    • Ignore any additional console output.
name: task_000000_67fdc8c3
